Here is the complete TODO report for the **webapp** project:

---

## 📋 TODO Report — `projects/webapp`

**2 TODO items found across 2 files**

---

### 1. `docs/README.md` — Line 28: `## TODO` (Backlog section)

This is the project's own tracked backlog. It lists **4 planned work items**:

| # | Work Item |
|---|-----------|
| 1 | **Add database persistence** — the app currently uses in-memory storage only |
| 2 | **Add authentication middleware** — no auth layer exists yet |
| 3 | **Add input validation on POST** — POST request bodies are not validated |
| 4 | **Add pagination for user listing** — the GET users endpoint returns all records |

---

### 2. `src/UsersController.scala` — Line 20: inline code TODO

```scala
case "POST" =>
    // TODO: parse body, validate, add user   ← line 20
    Response(201, """{"status":"created"}""")
```

**Work item:** The `POST /users` handler is a stub. It always returns `201 Created` without actually:
- Parsing the request body
- Validating the incoming data
- Adding the new user to the store

This directly corresponds to backlog items #3 & #1 from the README.

---

## Summary

| Priority | Item | Location |
|----------|------|----------|
| 🔴 High | Implement POST body parsing, validation & user creation | `UsersController.scala:20` |
| 🟠 Medium | Add database persistence (replace in-memory store) | README backlog |
| 🟠 Medium | Add authentication middleware | README backlog |
| 🟡 Low | Add input validation on POST routes | README backlog |
| 🟡 Low | Add pagination for user listing endpoint | README backlog |

The most actionable item is the incomplete `POST` handler in `UsersController.scala`, which is the only code-level TODO. The rest are architectural improvements tracked in the README backlog.
